_meta.name: CLM 1B r4 — RunPod H100 pod-create spec
_meta.purpose: Single-shot pod for CLM 1B r4 training (5.38 GB corpus, 750K steps, target eval<=1.0)
fire_with: # Method B (fallback — CLI flags, mirror the same spec):

name: anima-clm1b-r4

_capacity_rationale.gpuCount: 1 x H100 80GB sufficient: 1.51B bf16 model = ~3 GB, optimizer = ~12 GB, grads = ~3 GB, activations + cuda overhead ~3 GB -> 21 GB used, 59 GB headroom (per clm_1b_config.json vram_estimate)
_capacity_rationale.containerDiskInGb: 100 GB (vs 50 default): 5.38 GB corpus + 5.38 GB .gz upload + 3 GB model + 12 GB optimizer + ckpt rotation (3 ckpts x 18 GB during save = 54 GB transient peak); 100 GB gives ~30 GB headroom over peak. r3 used 50 GB and hit MFS quota at step 2000 (CLAUDE.md runpod_mfs_quota).
_capacity_rationale.volumeInGb: 0 (use container disk only — persistent volume billed separately, training is single-shot per One-Shot Best rule)
_capacity_rationale.minMemoryInGb: 64 (vs 32 default): byte-level corpus 5 GB needs ~10 GB RAM for chunked reads + dataloader; train_clm_1b.py keeps token windows in CPU memory before GPU dispatch. 32 GB was tight on r3f.
_capacity_rationale.vcpuCount: 16: dataloader + tokenization parallelism; H100 SXM nodes typically have 16-24 vCPU per GPU.
_capacity_rationale.secureCloud: true: required for stable bf16 + cuBLAS workspace (community cloud has been unstable for long-running CLM)
_capacity_rationale.supportPublicIp: true: needed for SSH/SCP corpus upload
_capacity_rationale.ports: 22/tcp only: no Jupyter, no extra services (R12 pod-stop-loses-gpu — fewer surfaces)

_cost_estimate.wall_hours_note: option B = 750K steps @ ~196 ms/step measured on r3f -> 750000 * 0.196 / 3600 = 40.83 h. Pad +0.2 h for setup/teardown.
_cost_estimate.cost_chosen_note: option B; option C (1.5M steps, 82 h, $246) is fallback if eval stalls > 1.0 at step 750K.

_post_create_steps: 1. runpodctl ssh info <pod_id>            # get ip + port
4. # Stage trainer + corpus + launcher (in this order; corpus is the slow one):
# Compress + upload corpus (5.38 GB raw -> ~2 GB gz, much faster scp):
5. # Install hexa binary (or skip if launcher is run via bash directly — see notes below):
# OR (simpler): the launcher is a thin wrapper; you can extract the bash payload and run it directly.
6. # Fire training in tmux/nohup (R11 nohup-check-disk + R10 verify-script-exists):
_abort_criteria: MFS quota dd preflight FAILS -> pod is poisoned, terminate and create new pod
GPU not H100 (unlikely with secureCloud + gpuTypeId set) -> launcher refuses, terminate
First 100 steps loss not decreasing OR loss NaN/Inf -> abort, investigate (LR? bf16 numerics?)
Eval at step 50000 (~20% of plan) > 2.0 -> stop, re-evaluate corpus quality
Wall clock > 60h with eval still > 1.1 -> stop early, save best ckpt, switch to option C in next round
Pod becomes unresponsive (R12) -> R2 last-known-good ckpt, do NOT pod-stop, terminate-and-rebuild

_post_training_r2_upload.wait_for: training process exit (no in-training upload — runpod_mfs_quota mandatory rule)

_teardown.trigger: training_summary.json present AND R2 upload verified AND eval results captured
_teardown.principle: Idle 0s -> immediate teardown after R2 verify. r3f cost $1.64 for 32 min; r4 = $122 for 41 h.
